hero.motto: Leading to excellence in Torah-Zionist education.

about.lede: Ulpenat Tzvia Kokhav Ya'akov is a religious-Zionist ulpana for young women — a community where the beit midrash, the lab, and the studio share the same blueprint.
about.body1: Founded in 1998 in Kokhav Ya'akov, Tzvia has grown into one of the leading religious-Zionist ulpanot in Mateh Binyamin. Our educational philosophy integrates deep Torah study with academic excellence and personal development.
about.body2: Our faculty brings together outstanding women scholars in Torah, leading scientists, and experienced educators — creating an environment where each student is challenged and supported to reach her highest potential.
about.body3: Graduates go on to national service, higher education, and careers in every field, carrying with them a life rooted in Torah and a mind sharpened by years of serious study.
about.pullQuote: We don't teach our students to choose between Torah and the world — we teach them that the world is where Torah is studied most honestly.

items.title: New STEM wing breaks ground next semester
items.excerpt: A 1,200 sqm addition with new labs, a makerspace, and a dedicated robotics studio.

items.title: Tenth grade takes national Tanakh quiz
items.excerpt: For the second consecutive year, our team brought home the regional title.

items.title: Open House — June 4th, 17:00
items.excerpt: Prospective students and families are welcome to tour campus and meet faculty.

items.title: Spotlight — Dr. R. Cohen on neuroscience
items.excerpt: From Tzvia to Tel Aviv University — research, family, meaning.

academy.intro: Life at Tzvia extends far beyond the classroom — a full programme of activities, journeys, and disciplines that shape each student as a whole person.

activities.name: Israeli Thought
activities.desc: Exploring Zionist philosophy, Jewish identity, and contemporary Israeli society through text, dialogue, and debate.

activities.name: Theatre
activities.desc: Acting, directing, and stagecraft — culminating in an annual production that brings the whole school together.

activities.name: National Excursions
activities.desc: Journeys across Israel — from the Galilee to the Negev — connecting classroom learning to the living landscape.

activities.name: Social Sciences
activities.desc: Sociology, psychology, and civic studies that teach students to read society as clearly as they read a text.

activities.name: Science & Technology
activities.desc: Lab-based biology, chemistry, physics, and a dedicated robotics track — building the scientists of tomorrow.

activities.name: Sports
activities.desc: Team sports, fitness, and inter-grade competitions in our full-court hall — because a strong mind needs a strong body.

activities.name: Visual Arts
activities.desc: Painting, sculpture, photography, and design — a studio where every student finds her visual voice.

calendar.noEventsMsg: No upcoming events scheduled for this week.

events.title: Open House — Campus tour
events.desc: Prospective students and families are welcome to tour the campus and meet our faculty.

events.title: Tanakh Bowl — Final round
events.desc: The annual inter-grade Tanakh competition. Students and staff welcome to attend.

events.title: Yom HaShavua · Communal learning
events.desc: Our weekly day of whole-school study — Torah, creative arts, and community building.

events.title: Spring concert
events.desc: An evening of music from the school's vocal ensemble, orchestra, and chamber groups.

events.title: 12th grade matriculation
events.desc: Graduation ceremony for the class of 5786. Families and alumnae warmly invited.

events.title: Closing assembly
events.desc: The year-end gathering marking the close of 5786 — with awards and reflections.

contact.h3: Come visit us on campus, or send a note.
contact.lede: We're a 25-minute drive from Jerusalem. Campus tours run Tuesdays and Thursdays — sign up below or write the office directly.
contact.placeholderMessage: Tell us a little about yourself or your daughter — and how we can help.

footer.brandDesc: A religious-Zionist ulpana for young women at the heart of Mateh Binyamin. Twenty-eight years of scholarship, community, and quiet excellence.
footer.newsletterHint: A quarterly note from the head of school — community, scholarship, and what's next.

academyPage.intro: A full programme of activities, academic disciplines, and enrichment tracks — designed to develop each student as a whole person.
academyPage.advisorDesc: Each programme has different commitments and entry points. We can help you find the right fit.

categories.label: ARTS
categories.desc: Stage, canvas, and instrument — disciplines where attention becomes craft.

categories.label: SCIENCE & TECH
categories.desc: Laboratories, makerspace, and online tracks — inquiry as a daily discipline.

categories.label: SPORTS
categories.desc: Team sports, conditioning, and individual disciplines across all grades.

categories.label: HUMANITIES
categories.desc: Text, history, and thought — the disciplines that shape how we read everything else.

newsArchive.desc: Announcements, achievements, events, and alumnae spotlights — the full archive of life at Tzvia.
